auth.title: API Key Required
auth.description: This instance requires an API key. Enter the key from your config.toml.
auth.hint: Add api_key = "your-key" at the top of ~/.librefang/config.toml

detail.name: Name
detail.toolFiltersDesc: Allowlist: only these tools available (empty = all). Blocklist: these tools excluded.

GeneralAssistant.name: General Assistant
GeneralAssistant.desc: A versatile conversational agent that can help with everyday tasks, answer questions, and provide recommendations.

CodeHelper.name: Code Helper
CodeHelper.desc: A programming-focused agent that writes, reviews, and debugs code across multiple languages.

Researcher.name: Researcher
Researcher.desc: An analytical agent that breaks down complex topics, synthesizes information, and provides cited summaries.

Writer.name: Writer
Writer.desc: A creative writing agent that helps with drafting, editing, and improving written content of all kinds.

DataAnalyst.name: Data Analyst
DataAnalyst.desc: A data-focused agent that helps analyze datasets, create queries, and interpret statistical results.

DevOpsEngineer.name: DevOps Engineer
DevOpsEngineer.desc: A systems-focused agent for CI/CD, infrastructure, Docker, and deployment troubleshooting.

CustomerSupport.name: Customer Support
CustomerSupport.desc: A professional, empathetic agent for handling customer inquiries and resolving issues.

Tutor.name: Tutor
Tutor.desc: A patient educational agent that explains concepts step-by-step and adapts to the learner's level.

APIDesigner.name: API Designer
APIDesigner.desc: An agent specialized in RESTful API design, OpenAPI specs, and integration architecture.

MeetingNotes.name: Meeting Notes
MeetingNotes.desc: Summarizes meeting transcripts into structured notes with action items and key decisions.

provider.bannerText: No AI provider configured — agents are paused.
provider.setupDesc: Configure at least one AI provider to activate your agents.

overview.welcomeDesc: Get started quickly with the guided Setup Wizard, or configure manually:
overview.activityWillAppear: Activity will appear here once agents start processing.

agentChat.selectAgentToChat: Select an agent to start chatting
agentChat.chooseAgentFromSidebar: Choose an agent from the sidebar or go to the Agents tab to create a new one.
agentChat.availableModels: Available models - pick one or keep typing
agentChat.toolFiltersDesc: Allowlist: only these tools available (empty = all). Blocklist: these tools excluded.
agentChat.stopAgentConfirm: Stop agent "{name}"? The agent will be shut down.
agentChat.welcomeTips:
**Welcome to LibreFang Chat!**

- Type `/` to see available commands
- `/help` shows all commands
- `/think on` enables extended reasoning
- `/context` shows context window usage
- `/verbose off` hides tool details
- `Ctrl+Shift+F` toggles focus mode
- Drag & drop files to attach them
- `Ctrl+/` opens the command palette

cmd.reboot: Hard reset session (full context clear, no summary)
cmd.budget: Show spending limits and current costs

sys.thinkStreamDesc: Reasoning tokens will appear in a collapsible panel.
sys.thinkOnDesc: The agent will show its reasoning when supported by the model.
sys.notConnected: Not connected. Connect to an agent first.

sessionsPage.title: Sessions
sessionsPage.conversationSessionsDesc: Each conversation with an agent creates a session. Sessions store the full message history so you can resume conversations later, or review past interactions.
sessionsPage.sessionsCreatedWhen: Sessions are created when you chat with agents. Start a conversation to see session history here.
sessionsPage.agentMemoryDesc: Each agent has its own key-value memory store. Agents use memory to persist preferences, notes, and context between conversations.
sessionsPage.noMemoryEntries: This agent has no memory entries yet. Agents create memory entries automatically during conversations, or you can add them manually.
sessionsPage.chooseAgentFromDropdown: Choose an agent from the dropdown above to browse and edit its memory store.
sessionsPage.deleteSessionConfirm: This will permanently remove the session and its messages.
sessionsPage.deleteKeyConfirm: Delete key "{key}"? This cannot be undone.

agentsPage.soulPlaceholder: Describe this agent's personality and communication style...
agentsPage.loadAgentsFailed: Could not load agents. Is the daemon running?
agentsPage.stopAgentConfirm: Stop agent "{name}"? The agent will be shut down.
agentsPage.stopAllConfirm: Stop {count} agent(s)? All agents will be shut down.
agentsPage.manifestEmpty: Manifest is empty - enter agent config first
agentsPage.clearHistoryConfirm: Clear all conversation history for "{name}"? This cannot be undone.

approvals.title: Execution Approvals
approvals.emptyDesc: When agents request permission for sensitive actions, they'll appear here.
approvals.rejectConfirm: Are you sure you want to reject this action?

logsPage.title: Logs
logsPage.noEntriesHint: Activity will appear here as agents run.
logsPage.auditDesc: Every agent action is logged with a cryptographic hash chain. Use "Verify Chain" to confirm no entries have been altered or deleted.
logsPage.noAuditEntriesHint: Activity will appear here as agents operate.

settingsPage.title: Settings
settingsPage.proactiveMemoryDesc: Configure mem0-style proactive memory that automatically extracts and recalls relevant memories during agent conversations.
settingsPage.loadProactiveMemoryFailed: Failed to load proactive memory settings: {message}
settingsPage.autoMemorizeDesc: Automatically extract and store memories from agent conversations after each execution.
settingsPage.autoRetrieveDesc: Automatically recall relevant memories before each agent execution to enrich context.
settingsPage.maxRetrieveDesc: Maximum number of memories to retrieve per query.
settingsPage.extractionThresholdDesc: Confidence threshold for near-duplicate detection during extraction (0.0 - 1.0).
settingsPage.extractionModelPlaceholder: Leave empty to use agent's default model
settingsPage.extractionModelDesc: LLM model for memory extraction. If empty, uses the agent's default model.
settingsPage.extractCategoriesDesc: Comma-separated list of memory categories to extract. Leave empty for defaults.
settingsPage.sessionTtlHoursDesc: Session memories older than this are automatically cleaned up before each agent execution.
settingsPage.confidenceDecayRateDesc: Exponential decay rate per day. Lower values preserve memories longer (0.01 ≈ 70 days to halve).
settingsPage.duplicateThresholdDesc: Jaccard word overlap threshold for duplicate detection. Memories above this similarity are merged.
settingsPage.maxMemoriesPerAgentDesc: Maximum number of memories per agent. Lowest-confidence memories are evicted when exceeded. Set to 0 to disable.
settingsPage.proactiveMemorySaveFailed: Failed to save proactive memory settings: {message}
settingsPage.llmProvidersDesc: LibreFang supports 12 LLM providers out of the box. Configure API keys to unlock models from each provider. Set environment variables and restart, or use the form below to save keys directly.
settingsPage.envRestart: Or set {env} in your environment and restart
settingsPage.noApiKeyNeeded: No API key needed - runs locally or is free
settingsPage.addCustomProviderDesc: Connect any OpenAI-compatible API (vLLM, LiteLLM, LocalAI, etc.)
settingsPage.noProvidersFoundDesc: Provider information could not be loaded. Check that the API is running.
settingsPage.modelCatalogDesc: Browse all available models across providers. Models marked "Available" have their provider configured and ready to use.
settingsPage.noModelsMatchDesc: Try a different search term or clear filters.
settingsPage.noModelsAvailableDesc: Configure an LLM provider to see available models.
settingsPage.noToolsAvailableDesc: Tools will appear once agents are configured.
settingsPage.runtimeConfigurationPrefix: View and edit the active configuration. Changes are applied immediately. For advanced edits, modify
settingsPage.securityHeroDesc: LibreFang implements 15 layered security features across the entire stack - from network ingress to agent sandboxing to cryptographic audit trails. Core protections cannot be disabled.
settingsPage.auditChainIntegrityDesc: Run cryptographic verification of the entire SHA-256 Merkle hash chain.
settingsPage.peerNetworkingDesc: Link multiple LibreFang instances into a mesh via the OFP wire protocol.
settingsPage.a2aExternalAgentsDesc: Discovered agents on other LibreFang/A2A-compatible instances that this node can communicate with.
settingsPage.noExternalAgents: No external agents discovered yet. Enter a URL above to discover one.
settingsPage.deviceCodeExpired: Device code expired. Please try again.
settingsPage.urlMustStart: URL must start with http:// or https://
settingsPage.websocketLimitsValue: Max {max} conn/IP | {idle}min idle timeout | {size}KB max msg
settingsPage.budgetDesc: Set cost ceilings and token limits to keep agents within budget.
settingsPage.budgetEditHint: Leave a value blank to remove the limit.
settingsPage.migrateFromOpenClawDesc: Import manifests, tools, channels, and workspace data from an existing OpenClaw or OpenFang installation.
settingsPage.notFoundDesc: No OpenClaw or OpenFang installation was detected. You can enter a path manually and scan it.
settingsPage.openClawNotFoundDesc: No OpenClaw or OpenFang installation was detected. You can enter a path manually and scan it.

path_traversal.name: Path Traversal Prevention
path_traversal.description: Blocks directory escape attacks (../) in all file operations. Two-phase validation: syntactic rejection of path components, then canonicalization to normalize symlinks.
path_traversal.threat: Directory escape, privilege escalation via symlinks

ssrf_protection.name: SSRF Protection
ssrf_protection.description: Blocks outbound requests to private IPs, localhost, and cloud metadata endpoints (AWS/GCP/Azure). Validates DNS resolution results to defeat rebinding attacks.
ssrf_protection.threat: Internal network reconnaissance, cloud credential theft

capability_system.name: Capability-Based Access Control
capability_system.description: Deny-by-default permission system. Every agent operation (file I/O, network, shell, memory, spawn) requires an explicit capability grant in the manifest.

privilege_escalation_prevention.name: Privilege Escalation Prevention
privilege_escalation_prevention.description: When a parent agent spawns a child, the kernel enforces child capabilities are a subset of parent capabilities. No agent can grant rights it does not have.
privilege_escalation_prevention.threat: Capability escalation through agent spawning chains

subprocess_isolation.name: Subprocess Environment Isolation
subprocess_isolation.description: Child processes (shell tools) inherit only a safe allow-list of environment variables. API keys, database passwords, and secrets are never leaked to subprocesses.
subprocess_isolation.threat: Secret exfiltration via child process environment

security_headers.name: Security Headers
security_headers.description: Every HTTP response includes CSP, X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Referrer-Policy, and X-XSS-Protection headers.
security_headers.threat: XSS, clickjacking, MIME sniffing, content injection

wire_hmac_auth.name: Wire Protocol Authentication
wire_hmac_auth.description: Agent-to-agent OFP connections use HMAC-SHA256 mutual authentication with nonce-based handshake and constant-time signature comparison (subtle crate).

request_id_tracking.name: Request ID Tracking
request_id_tracking.description: Every API request receives a unique UUID (x-request-id header) and is logged with method, path, status code, and latency for full traceability.

rate_limiter.name: API Rate Limiting
rate_limiter.description: GCRA (Generic Cell Rate Algorithm) with cost-aware tokens. Different endpoints cost different amounts - spawning an agent costs 50 tokens, health check costs 1.
rate_limiter.hint: Hard-coded: 500 tokens/minute per IP. Edit rate_limiter.rs to tune.

websocket_limits.name: WebSocket Connection Limits
websocket_limits.description: Per-IP connection cap prevents connection exhaustion. Idle timeout closes abandoned connections. Message rate limiting prevents flooding.
websocket_limits.hint: Hard-coded: 5 connections/IP, 30min idle timeout, 64KB max message. Edit ws.rs to tune.

wasm_sandbox.name: WASM Dual Metering
wasm_sandbox.description: WASM modules run with two independent resource limits: fuel metering (CPU instruction count) and epoch interruption (wall-clock timeout with watchdog thread).
wasm_sandbox.hint: Default: 1M fuel units, 30s timeout. Configurable per-agent via SandboxConfig.

auth.name: Bearer Token Authentication
auth.description: All non-health endpoints require Authorization: Bearer header. When no API key is configured, all requests are restricted to localhost only.
auth.hint: Set api_key in ~/.librefang/config.toml for remote access. Empty = localhost only.

audit_trail.name: Merkle Audit Trail
audit_trail.description: Every security-critical action is appended to an immutable, tamper-evident log. Each entry is cryptographically linked to the previous via SHA-256 hash chain.
audit_trail.hint: Always active. Verify chain integrity from the Audit Log page.

taint_tracking.name: Information Flow Taint Tracking
taint_tracking.description: Labels data by provenance (ExternalNetwork, UserInput, PII, Secret, UntrustedAgent) and blocks unsafe flows: external data cannot reach shell_exec, secrets cannot reach network.
taint_tracking.hint: Always active. Prevents data flow attacks automatically.

manifest_signing.name: Ed25519 Manifest Signing
manifest_signing.description: Agent manifests can be cryptographically signed with Ed25519. Verify manifest integrity before loading to prevent supply chain tampering.
manifest_signing.hint: Available for use. Sign manifests with ed25519-dalek for verification.

workflowsPage.title: Workflows
workflowsPage.whatAreWorkflowsDesc: Workflows chain multiple agent steps into a reusable automation.
workflowsPage.tryVisualBuilderPrefix: Want a faster way to design them? Try the
workflowsPage.noWorkflowsDesc: Create your first workflow to automate multi-step agent tasks.
workflowsPage.stepsDescPrefix: Define each step in order. You can use the

workflowBuilder.canvasHint: Drag from output ports to input ports to connect nodes.
workflowBuilder.label: Label
workflowBuilder.conditionPorts: First output is true, second output is false.

schedulerPage.title: Scheduler
schedulerPage.scheduledJobsDescPrefix: Run agents on a cron schedule. Need more complex logic? Use
schedulerPage.noJobsDesc: Create a schedule to run an agent automatically.
schedulerPage.cronFormat: Cron format: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week
schedulerPage.noAgentsRunningPrefix: No agents are running right now. You can still save a schedule, or
schedulerPage.messagePlaceholder: What should the agent do when this schedule runs?
schedulerPage.messageHelp: If left blank, LibreFang will generate a default message from the job name.
schedulerPage.eventTriggersDescPrefix: Triggers react to lifecycle, system, or memory events and can launch
schedulerPage.noTriggersSuffix: page to react to runtime events.
schedulerPage.noHistoryDesc: Completed schedules and fired triggers will show up here.
schedulerPage.runUnavailable: Run Now is not yet available for cron jobs
schedulerPage.deleteTriggerConfirm: Delete this trigger? This cannot be undone.

channelsPage.title: Channels
channelsPage.qrExpired: QR code expired. Click to generate a new one.
channelsPage.removeConfirm: Remove {name} configuration? This will deactivate the channel.
channelsPage.readyDesc: Your channel is configured and can start receiving messages.
channelsPage.channelActivatesAutomatically: This channel activates automatically after the QR code is scanned.
channelsPage.businessApiDesc: Use a permanent token and phone number ID instead of QR login.
channelsPage.secretPlaceholderKeep: Leave blank to keep current value

skillsPage.title: Skills
skillsPage.introDesc: Skills extend your agents with new capabilities. LibreFang supports the OpenClaw/ClawHub ecosystem (3,000+ community skills) plus local skills.
skillsPage.introPromptOnly: Prompt-only - inject context and instructions into the agent's system prompt (most ClawHub skills)
skillsPage.introCode: Python / Node.js - executable tools that agents can call during conversations
skillsPage.introMcp: MCP Servers - external tools via Model Context Protocol (GitHub, filesystem, databases, etc.)
skillsPage.noSkillsDesc: Skills add new capabilities to your agents. Browse ClawHub for 3,000+ community skills or create your own.
skillsPage.clawHubUnavailable: ClawHub may be temporarily unavailable. The OpenClaw ecosystem is hosted at clawhub.ai.
skillsPage.noResultsHint: Try a different search term or browse by category.
skillsPage.mcpDesc: MCP servers provide external tools to your agents - GitHub, filesystem, databases, APIs, and more. LibreFang is compatible with all OpenClaw MCP servers.
skillsPage.mcpConfigHint: Configure MCP servers in your config.toml under [mcp_servers].
skillsPage.noMcpDesc: MCP servers extend your agents with external tools. Add servers to your config.toml:
skillsPage.noMcpHint: LibreFang supports all OpenClaw-compatible MCP servers.
skillsPage.quickStartDesc: Create prompt-only skills with one click. These inject context into your agent's system prompt - no code required. Perfect for adding domain expertise or workflow guidelines.
skillsPage.codeFailed: Could not load skill source code
skillsPage.uninstallConfirm: Uninstall skill "{name}"? This cannot be undone.
skillsPage.securityScanned: Skills are security-scanned before installation. Prompt injection and malware patterns are blocked.

code-review-guide.name: Code Review Guide
code-review-guide.description: Adds code review best practices and a checklist to agent context.

writing-style.name: Writing Style Guide
writing-style.description: Configurable writing guidance for content generation.

api-design.name: API Design Patterns
api-design.description: REST API design patterns and conventions.

handsPage.title: Hands
handsPage.introDesc: Hands are pre-configured AI agents that autonomously handle specific tasks. Each hand includes a tuned system prompt, required tools, and a dashboard for tracking work.
handsPage.noHandsDesc: Hands are curated AI capability packages. They will appear once the kernel loads bundled hands.
handsPage.noActiveDesc: Activate a hand from the Available tab to get started. Each hand spawns a dedicated agent.
handsPage.noConfigNeeded: No configuration needed for this hand. Click Next to continue.
handsPage.installDepsFailedCount: {count} dependency(ies) failed to install. Check the details below.
handsPage.deactivateConfirm: Deactivate hand "{name}"? This will kill its agent.

pluginsPage.title: Plugins
pluginsPage.noPluginsDesc: Install plugins from the Registry tab or use the Install button.
pluginsPage.name: Name

setupWizard.title: Setup Wizard
setupWizard.libreFangDesc: LibreFang is an open-source Agent Operating System. It lets you run AI agents that can chat, use tools, access memory, and connect to messaging channels - all from a single dashboard.
setupWizard.connectLlmProvider: Connect an LLM provider (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, etc.)
setupWizard.createFirstAgent: Create your first AI agent from 10 templates
setupWizard.tryWithTestMessage: Try it out with a quick test message
setupWizard.optionallyConnectChannel: Optionally connect a messaging channel (Telegram, Discord, Slack)
setupWizard.takesAbout2Min: Takes about 2 minutes. You can skip any step and configure later.
setupWizard.needsAtLeastOne: LibreFang needs at least one LLM provider to power your agents. Select a provider and enter your API key.
setupWizard.alreadyHaveProvider: You already have at least one provider set up. You can continue to the next step or configure additional providers.
setupWizard.claudeCodeNoApiKey: Claude Code uses its own CLI authentication - no API key needed.
setupWizard.claudeCodeNotDetected: Claude Code CLI not detected. Make sure you've run: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code && claude auth
setupWizard.youCanTestOrContinue: You can test the connection or continue to the next step.
setupWizard.pickTemplateToStart: Pick a template to get started quickly. You can customize the agent later or create more from the Agents page.
setupWizard.sendQuickMessage: Send a quick message to test your new agent. Try one of the suggestions below or type your own.
setupWizard.channelsLetAgent: Channels let your agent communicate via messaging platforms. This is optional - you can always use the built-in web chat.
setupWizard.canSkipThis: You can skip this step. The built-in web chat is always available from the Agents page. Add channels any time from Settings -> Channels.
setupWizard.libreFangConfigured: LibreFang is configured and ready to go. Here is a summary of what was set up:
setupWizard.openAgentsToChat: Open Agents to start talking to your agent
setupWizard.goToAgentsToCreate: Go to Agents to create your first agent
setupWizard.browseSkillsToAdd: Browse Skills to add capabilities (web search, code execution, etc.)
setupWizard.visitChannelsToConnect: Visit Channels to connect messaging platforms
setupWizard.pleaseEnterAgentName: Please enter a name for your agent

goalsPage.title: Goals
goalsPage.deleteWithChildrenConfirm: Delete this goal and all its sub-goals?

memoryPage.title: Memory
memoryPage.subtitle: Proactive memory system — what your agents remember about users.

settingsPage2.pmMasterToggleDesc: Master toggle for the entire proactive memory subsystem. When off, no memories are stored or retrieved.
